title: Pronouncing Experiences
learningGoal: Master the pronunciation of past participles and the contracted forms of 'have' (I've, you've).
instructions: 過去分詞（eaten, seenなど）の発音と、現在完了形で使われる「have」の短縮形（I've, you've）の発音を練習します。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice pronouncing past participles and contractions with 'have'. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 私は〜した（I have の短縮形）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Bite your bottom lip gently for the 'v' sound. I-v.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: あなたは〜した（you have の短縮形）
私たちは〜した（we have の短縮形）
彼らは〜した（they have の短縮形）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Tongue between the teeth for 'th'. Vay-v.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: ハヴュー エヴァー
targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 今までに〜したことがありますか

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: In American English, the 't' is often stopped in the throat. Ee-tn.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 行ったことがある、いた（beの過去分詞）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: In American English, it often sounds like the word 'bin'. B-i-n.
Sounds exactly like 'dun' (or the sun). D-uh-n.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 建てられた（buildの過去/過去分詞）

expectedPronunciationText: I've you've we've they've Have you ever eaten seen been done built

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! Your 'v' sounds in the contractions were excellent, and you pronounced the past participles clearly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Pay close attention to the 'v' sound in 'I've' and 'you've'. Don't say 'bu'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember that 'done' rhymes with 'sun', not 'bone'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: I've you've we've they've Have you ever eaten seen been done built
acceptedVariants: I have you have we have they have have you ever eaten seen been done built
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: I've you've we've they've Have you ever eaten seen been done built
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: ive yoov weev vayv havyooehver eetn seen bin dun bilt

audioAssets.audioTranscript: I've, you've, we've, they've, Have you ever, eaten, seen, been, done, built

hintText: Make sure to make a 'v' sound (biting your bottom lip) for I've and you've.
